description: A yellow-green empidonax, perched high in the cottonwoods.  Looking up I noticed deep yellow throat, pale, wide lower mandible and yellow overall cast to head and underside.  The wings were dark with off-white wingbars.  The secondary edges were white and stopped well short of the lower wingbar.  The primary projection was medium. A complete white to off-white, circular eyering was evident.  The head was round and the bird looked plump, like a Least Flycatcher.  The bird was observed for several minutes from 15 - 30 feet away by qualified birders who all agreed on ID after careful study and photos.
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
